title: Database Management System (DBMS) Architecture & Languages
description: A high-level overview of the Three-Schema DBMS Architecture and how the different database languages interact with each layer.
relationships: DML/Query Language is used by End Users/Programs to interact with the External Level
DDL is used by DBA to define the Conceptual Schema
DCL is used by DBA to define security/access at the Internal and External Levels
Conceptual Level maps to the Internal Level
Internal Level describes physical storage structures
